revision.text:
[[ja:羽黒神社 (長岡市小貫)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Haguro Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Nagaoka, Niigata}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|en|Nagaoka, Niigata|qid=Q540312|lt=Nagaoka City|1=Nagaoka, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.49188888888889|N|138.97505555555554|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Haguro Gongen]]{{ill|Haguro Gongen|ja|羽黒権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Gongen|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Ungraded shrine (無格社) | founded = 1731 (Kyōhō 12) | main_hall = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] | other_names = | annual_festival = | rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Nagaoka|qid=Q540312|1=Nagaoka, Niigata}} }} '''Haguro Shrine''' (羽黒神社, *Haguro Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Onuki, Nagaoka City, Niigata Prefecture, Japan'''. It is said to have been '''founded in 1731 (Kyōhō 12)''' by the influential '''Toyama family''' of the region. On the shrine grounds, there is a '''commemorative monument''' dedicated to '''Toyama Shuzō''', the founder of '''Asahi Breweries''' and '''Hanshin Electric Railway'''This monument was created later based on an original monument at his grave in Osaka.. == '''Legends''' == A local legend tells that '''Otoha Gozen''', the mother of the '''Sato brothers''', {{ill|Satō Tsugunobu|en|Satō Tsugunobu|qid=Q11384341|lt=Sato Tsugunobu|1=Satō Tsugunobu}} and {{ill|Satō Tadanobu|en|Satō Tadanobu|qid=Q1711419|lt=Sato Tadanobu|1=Satō Tadanobu}}, both '''loyal retainers of Minamoto no Yoshitsune''', was deeply grieving their deaths. She received a '''divine revelation''' instructing her to '''enshrine Haguro Gongen from the Three Mountains of Dewa (Dewa Sanzan) in Echigo Province''', leading to the shrine’s foundation{{Cite web|title=Haguro Shrine in Nagaoka – A Shrine Established by Otoha to Mourn Her Sons|url=https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0004230046_00000|publisher=[[NHK]] – Shin Nihon Kikō Archives|year=2005|accessdate=2019-07-10}}. Additionally, in '''Izumozaki Town, Niigata''', there is another '''legend related to Otoha Gozen'''. It is said that, while traveling from Kyoto to '''Yashima''' to mourn her sons, she '''built a hermitage in Izumozaki''', which is connected to the '''origin of the Okesa-bushi''' folk song{{ill|Sado Okesa|en|Sado Okesa|qid=Q7398020|lt=Sado Okesa|1=Sado Okesa}}. == '''Access''' == === '''Public Transport''' === The nearest bus stop is '''Onuki (小貫)''', serviced by '''Echigo Kotsu (越後交通)'''. The bus routes vary by line: * '''Rapid Service:''' Nagaoka Station → Kuwasagimine → Tochio * '''Express Service:''' Nagaoka Station → Mitsuke → Tochio (*via Onuki*) == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Related Topics''' == * [[Tochio, Nagaoka|Tochio (Nagaoka City)]] == '''External Links''' == * {{Wayback|date=20210213110200|url=http://tochiokankou.jp/bunka/jinjya.html|title=Shrines – Tochio Tourism Association}} {{Shinto-stub}} }} {{translated page|ja|羽黒神社 (長岡市小貫)|version=98250409|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:羽黒神社 (長岡市小貫)]] {{wikidata link|Q85883240}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagaoka, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagaoka, Niigata]] [[Category:Image Requests for Nagaoka City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
